name: artifacts-builder
description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
capabilities: Initialize React + Vite project with TypeScript
Install and configure Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui theming
Configure path aliases for clean imports
Bundle React app into single self-contained HTML file
Support dark mode and CSS variables theming
author: davila7
securitySummary: Legitimate frontend build tool for creating Claude.ai artifacts. Scripts initialize React projects, install npm packages via standard package managers, and bundle code into self-contained HTML. All static findings are false positives: license URLs are legitimate, shell commands are standard build tooling, and cryptographic alerts are triggered by version numbers and legal text, not actual crypto usage.
valueStatement: Creating complex interactive artifacts for Claude.ai requires modern frontend tooling. This skill sets up a complete React development environment with shadcn/ui components and bundles everything into a single HTML file ready to share in conversations.
userTitle: Build powerful React artifacts for Claude
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher on your system
Not for simple single-file HTML or JSX artifacts
Cannot create artifacts requiring server-side code

useCases.title: Interactive dashboards
useCases.description: Build data visualization dashboards with charts, tables, and real-time state management

useCases.title: UI component showcases
useCases.description: Create polished component libraries and interactive design systems with shadcn/ui components

useCases.title: Prototype applications
useCases.description: Build functional prototypes with routing, forms, and complex user flows

promptTemplates.title: Basic artifact
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the artifacts-builder skill to create a [describe your artifact idea]. Initialize the project, develop the UI with appropriate shadcn components, and bundle it into a single HTML file I can share as an artifact.

promptTemplates.title: Dashboard with data
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an interactive dashboard artifact using artifacts-builder. Include [list data sources or metrics]. Use appropriate charts, tables, and filtering controls. Make it visually clean with dark mode support.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-view application
promptTemplates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to create a [describe application] with multiple views and navigation. Include [list required components]. Bundle everything into a single artifact that feels like a complete application.

promptTemplates.title: Form-heavy interface
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a comprehensive form interface with validation using artifacts-builder. Include fields for [list data to collect]. Use react-hook-form with zod validation and display errors clearly. Style with shadcn/ui components.

bestPractices: Use shadcn/ui components consistently for a cohesive look across artifacts
Test artifacts in browser before sharing to catch rendering issues
Keep artifacts focused on a single purpose rather than trying to do too much
antiPatterns: Do not use this for simple HTML snippets that could be written directly
Avoid excessive animations or visual effects that slow down the artifact
Do not include external CDN links - bundle everything locally

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Free?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled HTML files work in any Claude plan. Only Claude Code uses the skill itself.

faq.question: What is the maximum artifact size?
faq.answer: Artifacts have no strict limit but smaller files load faster. Keep under 500KB for best performance.

faq.question: Can I use external APIs in artifacts?
faq.answer: Yes, but ensure CORS is enabled on the API. Client-side calls work if the server allows cross-origin requests.

faq.question: Is my data safe when building artifacts?
faq.answer: Yes. All processing happens locally on your machine. No data is sent to external servers during build.

faq.question: Why is my artifact not rendering correctly?
faq.answer: Check browser console for errors. Ensure all dependencies are bundled. Avoid server-side features like Node.js modules.

faq.question: How is this different from writing artifacts directly?
faq.answer: This enables complex state, routing, and 40+ pre-built components. Best for multi-view apps, not simple static content.
